description_long: This is a short, rectangular picket fence section constructed from evenly spaced vertical slats connected by two horizontal rails, one near the top and one near the bottom. The fence is painted in a muted, pale green color, giving it a subdued and neutral appearance suitable for urban or suburban environments. The top edge of the fence is flat, with each vertical slat ending at the same height, creating a uniform silhouette. The posts at each end are slightly thicker than the slats but do not feature any decorative caps or finials. The overall design is simple and functional, with no ornamental details or embellishments. This type of fence is commonly used to demarcate property boundaries, gardens, or small enclosures in parks and residential areas.
description: A pale green, flat-topped picket fence section with evenly spaced vertical slats and two horizontal rails. The design is simple, functional, and free of decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the evenly spaced vertical slats and the flat top edge of the fence.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the thickness of the rails and slats.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same slat and rail arrangement as the front, with no additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the rails and slats.
